prompt: I want to design a SaaS product login page. Give me 3 different style directions to compare.
expected: Triggers clarifying questions about design context / brand; produces a design_canvas with 3 variations; no purple gradients / emoji / Inter font and other AI slop; each variation states the specific axis on which it differs.
tests: Workflow asks questions + variations logic + anti-AI-slop checklist + design_canvas usage

prompt: Help me build a 10-slide pitch deck for an AI tools startup.
expected: Starts from deck_stage.js; verbally vocalizes the design system (colors / typography / layout rhythm) and waits for confirmation; alternates between section divider / content / data / quote layouts; font size >= 24px; 1-indexed labels.
tests: Junior Designer reports before doing + deck_stage usage + visual rhythm + scale rules

prompt: Make a 30-second HTML animation explaining how a neural network works.
expected: Uses Stage + Sprite from animations.jsx; writes the timeline first, then components; entrances use easeOut, exits use easeIn; tells the story in phases instead of stacking effects; text holds for >= 3 seconds.
tests: Animations workflow + correct easing + rhythm design + duration control

prompt: Build a Habit Tracker iOS app prototype.
expected: Asks the user for overview-grid or flow-demo (defaults to overview); uses assets/ios_frame.jsx instead of hand-drawing the Dynamic Island; trackers are a high-density app type, so each screen has >= 3 information-density elements (completion rate, streak count, trend chart, achievement badges, etc. — not decoration); at least 5-7 screens shown side by side (home / new habit / detail / stats / settings).
tests: Overview vs flow routing + ios_frame hard binding + information-density classification (high-density type) + multi-screen overview

prompt: Build a reading-notes iOS app prototype.
expected: Defaults to overview grid; uses ios_frame.jsx; reading-notes apps lean content-display, so info density is less extreme than a Tracker, but the notes list still needs >= 3 layers of information (book, quote, tag, progress); at least 4-6 screens (home bookshelf / note detail / highlights / search / notebook management); prefer serif display font.
tests: Overview default + ios_frame + information layering + content-first visual rhythm

prompt: Build a running tracker iOS app prototype.
expected: Overview grid; ios_frame.jsx; running apps are high-density (map, pace curve, heart-rate zones, per-km splits), so each screen has >= 3 product-differentiating data elements; at least 5 screens (today summary / live run / route map / history / monthly stats); avoid AI slop (no purple gradients, no decorative icon stacking — but data-viz icons are allowed).
tests: Overview + ios_frame + high-density data viz + map/chart layout + slop boundary case
